history indian institute technology redirecttologinpage
way visionary leaders country conceived idea establishing higher technical institutes support post war industrial development india
committee headed nalini ranjan sarkar recommended establishment institutes various parts india
first indian institute technology founded kharagpur
jawaharlal nehru first prime minister india first convocation kharagpur clarified address institutes technology would help meet technological challenges nation
four iits established bombay delhi kanpur madras
subsequently september parliament india passed act known institutes technology act declaring kharagpur institute national importance
kharagpur given status autonomous university
thereafter guwahati started university roorkee given status called roorkee
establishment eight iits across country began decision cabinet announced minister human resource development march
bhubaneswar gandhinagar hyderabad earlier called rajasthan patna ropar began functioning others indore mandi commenced sessions
past decades since iits become hallmark quality technology undergraduate education internationally attributed largely ambience quality academics
endeavor uphold brand image meticulously built last years renewed focus producing world class technology driven graduates contribute nation development

sustainability policy indian institute technology redirecttologinpage
sustainability policy unique master plan conceptualizes workings parts campus interlocking integral network complex dynamic systems like metabolism living organism
meta system actively studied monitored partly generate intelligent control instructions partly mine data sense settlement evolving trials tests living laboratory
ideas smart intelligent eco campus encompass ideals social economic environmental sustainability integrate aspects landscape biodiversity food water waste solid waste mobility energy ict create intricate life like system campus metabolism berms act signature bounding elements containing compact desert settlements
mitigate noise dust heat part desertification strategy along green buffer zones green infrastructure compact settlement patterns east west streets
campus sustainable oasis challenging desert context providing protected habitat flora fauna including humans
rejuvenates site providing biodiversity corridors allow native species contiguous habitat passage across site within region isolated island sanctuaries human settlement
landscape plan aims minimizing water requirement using recycled water
campus uses hardy native species plants conserving water improving soil moisture requiring little upkeep easy disease management
landscape designed absorb stormwater even extreme rainfall incidents prevent erosion flooding
landscape provides open space interaction students faculty local communities artists art installations public spaces suitable green cover parked vehicles
extremely water scarce area roof surface runoff stored cisterns
campus made zero water demanding campus storing runoff areas ground large cisterns merge saline aquifer tapped due located distressed zone
therefore possibility targeted zero water regime proposed modified create low water regime
water consumption campus reduced notably efficient fixtures semi automated irrigation systems native plantation extensive recycling reuse sewage rainwater harvesting storage recharge encouraging low water lifestyle
taking account treated water recharge seasons increased rainwater recharge campus net zero water campus
period years soil moisture improves salinity aquifer reduces remainder white water needs met groundwater thus making autonomous zero water demanding campus provided groundwater extraction allowed future
rainfall management system creates large storage capacity roof runoff water security
campus create zero solid waste regime segregating waste managing stream appropriately mixed waste landfill eliminated
campus create positive value recyclables paper glass reusable plastics metals isolates hazardous wastes chemical biological specialized disposal certified recyclers stacks waste small quantity mixed waste stores specialized disposal progressively establishing new research cluster waste mixed waste recycling increasing recycling capacity site
energy consumption campus reduced notably passive traditional techniques building integrated renewable energy technologies compact building clustering encouraging low energy lifestyle
buildings energy efficient low resource consuming buildings globally
use double wall system thermal insulation rooftop reduce heat load reduce energy consumption spaces provide adaptive conditioned comfort nearly habitable areas minimizing energy reducing water

lakhs start date sep end date sep outcome hydrogen inflammable gases several potential applications
due low ignition energy broad explosive range hydrogen safety precaution highly recommended
nowadays metal oxide based gas sensors broadly used due good performance low cost compatibility electronic circuits
however sensors drawback like poor selectivity high operating temperature
thus highly desirable improve sensing properties using transition metal doping decoration noble nanoparticles addition type material inclusion carbonaceous nanomaterials
development tunable filter based ferroelectric thin film sputtering indian national science academy mahesh kumar
